title: Robot Surveillance Sabotage
description: A steampunk surveillance camera patrols a restricted zone, sweeping its gaze back and forth. The player controls a robot that must sneak past the camera's cone of vision by timing movements, hacking terminal nodes, and ultimately disabling the camera to escape. The robot must reach the exit gate on the far side without being detected three times.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap with ES modules from cdn.jsdelivr.net). Load 'shared_assets/robot.glb' as the player character and 'shared_assets/steampunk_camera.glb' as a mounted surveillance device on a pole at the center of the scene. Scene setup: a dark industrial floor plane (30x30 units) with subtle grid texture, dim ambient light (0.15 intensity), and a red spotlight attached to the camera representing its detection cone. The steampunk_camera should rotate back and forth on its Y-axis in a sweeping 120-degree arc with a 4-second period. Render a translucent red cone mesh in front of the camera representing the visible detection zone. Place the robot at one edge and an exit gate (a glowing green wireframe arch) at the opposite edge. Scatter 4 hackable terminal nodes (small glowing cyan boxes) across the floor. Interaction steps: (1) WASD keys move the robot across the floor with smooth translation. (2) When the robot enters the camera's detection cone, flash the screen red and increment a strike counter displayed in an HTML overlay HUD. Three strikes trigger game over with a restart button. (3) Pressing E near a terminal node (within 2 units) initiates a 1.5-second hack animation (node pulses and changes to green). (4) After hacking exactly 2 terminals, the camera sweep speed doubles, increasing difficulty. (5) After hacking all 4 terminals, the camera's detection cone shrinks by 50%. (6) Moving the robot to the exit gate triggers a victory screen. Validate that terminals must be hacked in sequence (closest to farthest from start). Display a timer in the HUD. Add OrbitControls for optional camera angle adjustment with right-click only. The player camera should default to a top-down isometric view at 45 degrees. Add particle sparks when hacking terminals.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  robotPositionX: number,         // robot's X coordinate on the floor plane
  robotPositionZ: number,         // robot's Z coordinate on the floor plane
  cameraRotationY: number,         // current Y-axis rotation angle of the surveillance camera in radians
  strikeCount: number,         // how many times the robot has been detected (0-3)
  terminalsHacked: number,         // count of terminal nodes successfully hacked (0-4)
  isHacking: boolean,         // whether the robot is currently hacking a terminal
  gameOver: boolean,         // whether the game has ended due to three strikes
  gameWon: boolean,         // whether the robot has reached the exit gate successfully
  detectionConeActive: boolean,         // whether the camera's detection cone is currently checking for the robot
  exitGateDistance: number,         // distance in units from robot to the exit gate
  nearestTerminalDistance: number,         // distance in units to the closest unhacked terminal
}
